title: Harbor & Hearth

hero.headline: Harbor & Hearth
hero.subheadline: Wood-fired plates and a thoughtful bar on the waterfront

body:
# Welcome

We are a neighborhood restaurant focused on Maine seafood, local farms, and a bar program that favors small producers. The **menu** lists what is on the pass tonight — no public online ordering; staff run tabs and events through Square at the house.

## Tonight

- Raw bar rotation and daily catch
- A short list of wines by the glass
- Happy hour at the bar, Tuesday through Thursday, 4–6pm

Browse the **catalog** for current dishes and dietary notes, or inquire about a **private event** if you are planning a gathering.

title: Our story
body:
# About Harbor & Hearth

Chef Mara Ellis opened Harbor & Hearth in 2018 after a decade cooking on fishing boats and in oyster bars downeast. The dining room seats forty-six; the bar seats twelve. We buy whole fish when we can, break them down in-house, and build menus around what landed that week.

## What to expect

- Reservations recommended Thursday through Saturday
- Walk-ins welcome at the bar when seats are open
- We accommodate allergies with notice — tell your server before you order

Private buyouts and rehearsal dinners are common here; start with the **private event** form and we will follow up with a package.

title: Private events

hero.headline: Private events
hero.subheadline: Buyouts, seated dinners, and standing receptions

body:
# Private events

Harbor & Hearth hosts weddings, corporate dinners, and family celebrations. We offer full-restaurant buyouts on Sunday and Monday evenings, or partial dining-room holds on select weeknights.

## Typical packages

- **Seated dinner** — three courses, family-style sides, coffee service
- **Cocktail reception** — passed bites, raw bar add-on, open bar or consumption tab
- **Chef's lunch** — shorter menu for daytime meetings (twenty guests minimum)

Submit the **private event** form with your date range and headcount. Our events lead replies within two business days with availability and a rough per-guest estimate.
